title: Butter Chicken & Rice
description: A creamy, mild butter chicken served with fluffy basmati rice, perfect for a family dinner. This comforting meal is a kid-friendly favorite and can be made mostly in one pan.
instructions: Rinse basmati rice thoroughly. In a medium saucepan, combine rice and water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes, or until water is absorbed. Remove from heat and let stand, covered, for 5 minutes, then fluff with a fork.
While rice cooks, cut chicken thighs into 1-inch pieces. Season lightly with salt.
In a large skillet or Dutch oven, heat vegetable oil and 1 tablespoon of butter over medium heat. Add chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger to the skillet and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add chicken pieces to the skillet and cook until lightly browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in garam masala, turmeric, and cumin. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly, until fragrant.
Pour in crushed tomatoes, salt, and sugar. Bring to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 10-15 minutes, or until chicken is cooked through.
Stir in heavy cream and the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter until melted and well combined. Heat through for 2-3 minutes, but do not boil.
Serve the butter chicken immediately over the fluffy basmati rice.
tips: For a milder flavor suitable for younger children, reduce the amount of garam masala and omit the cumin.
Serve with warm naan bread or steamed green vegetables like broccoli or peas for a complete meal.
Leftover butter chicken can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days and reheats well.
